reason: Review end-to-end draft->published->wire->node runtime flow and catch format, validation, or state-owner drift across layers.
Check new handlers/controllers/components reuse existing shared contracts and do not introduce duplicated validators or payload parsing.
Verify additive portable migrations, indexes, transaction boundaries, and rollback behavior for Panel and node state.
Verify logical bucket deletion never removes native objects and managed routing preserves S3 path/list/error contracts.
Verify credential disable/quota and bucket ACL changes invalidate caches and take effect immediately without weakening auth.
Verify webhook runtime replacement preserves queue/retry/non-blocking behavior and only supported events are accepted.
Verify every Panel mutation is authenticated, validated, sanitized, secret-safe, and returns the expected HTTP error matrix.
Verify typed client/UI behavior, one-time secret handling, visible failures, shared style classes, build, responsive, and browser acceptance requirements.
